hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in HRM features PwC-taught modules (premium tier), AI in Talent Acquisition subject, zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. Designed for HR professionals targeting people analytics, HRBP, and talent management roles.

about.body:
Human Resource Management as an MBA specialisation has evolved beyond administrative HR into strategic people management, analytics-driven talent decisions, and AI-assisted workforce planning. Chandigarh University Online's HR programme reflects this shift with subjects covering HR analytics, AI in talent acquisition, compensation management, and organisation change.

The PwC partnership (Tier 2) is directly relevant to HR students: PwC's People and Organisation practice is one of its largest advisory services globally, covering HR transformation, talent strategy, compensation benchmarking, and future-of-work studies. Modules co-taught by PwC professionals bring consulting-grade HR frameworks into the curriculum.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, with NAAC A+ accreditation. The university's own large campus HR function and placement machinery give the online HR programme practical grounding. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not take commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Strategic HR alignment with business goals and workforce strategy in global organisations.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Data-driven decision models applied to HR problems including attrition, hiring, and performance.

subjects.name: Human Resource Analytics
subjects.desc: People data analysis, workforce metrics dashboards, attrition modelling, and HR ROI measurement.

subjects.name: Organisation Change and Development
subjects.desc: Change management models, OD interventions, culture transformation, and managing resistance.

subjects.name: AI in Talent Acquisition and Workforce Planning
subjects.desc: AI-driven recruiting tools, predictive workforce planning, and bias-mitigation in hiring.

subjects.name: Compensation Management
subjects.desc: Pay structure design, job evaluation, incentive schemes, and total rewards benchmarking.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: Project delivery frameworks applied to HR transformation initiatives and large-scale change programmes.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI tools for HR including performance prediction, automated engagement surveys, and workforce analytics.

subjects.name: Performance Management: Systems and Strategies
subjects.desc: Goal-setting frameworks (OKR, BSC), appraisal design, and continuous feedback systems.

subjects.name: Methodology of Training and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, instructional design, learning evaluation (Kirkpatrick), and L&D ROI.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Applied HR industry project on a real workforce or talent challenge, completed with a live partner.

whoHires.body:
HR MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across all industry sectors:

**Industries:** IT and technology, manufacturing, BFSI, FMCG, retail, consulting, logistics, healthcare, and government PSUs

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Training Coordinator, HR Business Partner (Junior)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, HRBP, L&D Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, HR Analytics Specialist

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, CHRO, Head of Talent Acquisition, VP People, Head of Organisation Development

**Top hiring organisations:** TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Deloitte, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, HUL, Amazon India, Flipkart, and large public sector employers where NAAC A+ online degrees carry government recognition

skills.body:
The HRM specialisation builds skills that People and Organisation employers test at HR Manager and HRBP levels:

**Technical skills:**
- HR Analytics: workforce metrics design, attrition prediction, HR dashboard creation, and people data storytelling
- Talent Acquisition: AI-assisted sourcing tools, structured interview frameworks, employer branding, and campus recruiting design
- Compensation Management: pay band design, benchmarking surveys, variable pay plan modelling, and total rewards architecture
- Performance Management: OKR implementation, continuous feedback system design, and calibration process management

**Applied skills:**
- Organisation Development: change readiness assessment, stakeholder mapping, intervention design, and change communication
- Training Design: TNA frameworks, blended learning architecture, and Level 3/4 impact measurement using Kirkpatrick

**Soft skills:** Influencing business leaders without direct authority, facilitating difficult conversations between managers and teams, interpreting people data for non-HR audiences, and building trust with employees across hierarchy levels.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA HRM against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity is NAAC A+, NIRF #22, strong brand recognition in HR hiring for Delhi NCR corporates. Fee is Rs 2,25,000. CU offers similar NAAC A+ credential at Rs 1,65,000 (Tier 1) with the PwC people analytics and HR consulting advantage at Tier 2. For candidates targeting consulting-grade HR roles, CU Tier 2 is the stronger profile.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA HR:** NMIMS carries a strong brand in BFSI HR and Mumbai-area corporate hiring. Fee is Rs 2,05,000-2,10,000. NMIMS is the better brand choice for candidates targeting BFSI HR or Mumbai-based companies. CU is the better value choice for professionals at Rs 1,65,000 who want the PwC partnership and North India network.

**vs Manipal Jaipur Online MBA HR:** MUJ has NAAC A+ and similar fee range. Neither has the other's specific industry partnership advantage. CU's PwC People and Organisation teaching is a clear differentiator for HR professionals targeting advisory, consulting, or analytics-led HR roles.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in Human Resource Management offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. The NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials make this certificate effective in both private sector and PSU HR hiring where accreditation is a shortlisting criterion.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC People and Organisation co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM preparation, strengthening profiles for HR consulting, people analytics, and OD roles. DigiLocker integration provides instant digital verification.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA HRM alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sunita B.
items.liked: AI in Talent Acquisition subject was practically useful for my current HRBP work
items.disliked: Compensation Management could go deeper into variable pay for IT sector roles
items.body: The AI in Talent Acquisition module changed how I approach sourcing. I went from manual JD-to-candidate matching to using predictive tools within 3 months. The PwC HR transformation framework (Tier 2) gave me consulting-quality language that resonated in my CHRO's team. Got promoted to Sr HRBP. Strongly recommend Tier 2 for HR professionals targeting advisory or analytics roles.

items.name: Mohit K.
items.liked: Harvard Business Publishing certificate and NAAC A+ both noticed in job applications
items.disliked: LMS user experience needs improvement for mobile learners
items.body: The Harvard Business Publishing certificate on my LinkedIn profile got noticed by three HR consulting firms within a month. My NAAC A+ credential from CU was checked and verified by two MNC HR hiring managers before they scheduled me. The combination works. LMS is functional but not the smoothest on mobile - recommend using a laptop for live sessions.

items.name: Preet K.
items.liked: Zero-cost EMI through university meant no credit card dependency
items.disliked: Tier options were confusing at enrolment - needed repeated clarification from admissions
items.body: The programme content was solid, especially HR Analytics and Organisation Change. The tier decision process was genuinely confusing - the admissions team needed to explain the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 three times before it clicked. Once enrolled in Tier 1 at Rs 1,65,000, the EMI through the university was smooth. CU could simplify the enrolment process significantly.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. This is unmatched among Indian online MBAs - most offer only generic curriculum. PwC-taught modules are particularly valuable for HR professionals seeking roles at consulting firms, Big 4 People and Organisation advisory, or HR analytics functions at large organisations. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and the same core MBA curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship bringing the net fee to Rs 1,65,000 with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship bringing the net fee to Rs 1,80,400 with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For HR professionals targeting OD consulting or people analytics, Tier 2 adds clear profile value.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure without routing through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current tenure options and eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Does the Chandigarh Online MBA HRM prepare for SHRM or CIPD certifications?
faqs.answer: The MBA HRM curriculum covers content areas that overlap with SHRM-CP (talent acquisition, compensation, training) and CIPD Foundation Level (HR practice, employment law, L&D). The MBA does not automatically prepare you for the SHRM or CIPD exams, but it builds the foundational knowledge you would build on for those certifications. Many HR professionals pursue SHRM-CP or CIPD after their MBA once they have the work experience requirement.

faqs.question: Is the AI in Talent Acquisition subject relevant for current HR roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. AI in Talent Acquisition and Workforce Planning covers AI-assisted sourcing, predictive hiring models, ATS optimisation, and bias-mitigation tools being actively deployed by mid-size to large organisations. Companies including TCS, Infosys, HDFC Bank, and Amazon India now use AI-assisted talent screening. This subject gives HR professionals practical familiarity with tools their employers are already using.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A+, NIRF #19 HRM credential at Rs 1,65,000 with the 25% Early Bird Scholarship.
You want PwC-taught HR consulting and people analytics content (Tier 2) alongside your degree.
You are targeting HRBP, people analytics, or OD roles that value consulting-grade HR frameworks.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university without NBFC intermediary.
consider: You specifically need NMIMS brand recognition for BFSI HR or Mumbai corporate hiring.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and you are open to NAAC A++ LPU at a lower fee point.
You need SHRM or CIPD certification path guidance - a specialist HR institute may suit better alongside an MBA.
